description_long: This object consists of two vintage metal kettles, both cylindrical in shape with open tops and side-mounted handles. One kettle is plain, made of dull, weathered metal with a slightly rough, matte finish and a utilitarian appearance. The other kettle is white with a black rim and features a large, colorful sunflower motif on its exterior, giving it a decorative touch. Both kettles lack visible lids and have flat bases, with the white kettle showing some rust and wear near the bottom. The handles are simple, looped, and attached to the side, suitable for gripping. These kettles are typical of older, utilitarian kitchenware and could be found in urban settings such as garages, workshops, or outdoor markets.
description: Two vintage metal kettles, one plain and weathered, the other white with a sunflower motif and black rim, both with side handles and open tops.
description_view_0: The open tops of both kettles are visible, highlighting the plain metal interior and the sunflower motif on the white kettle.
description_view_1: The side handles of both kettles are prominent, with the white kettle's handle and black rim clearly visible.
description_view_2: The bottoms of both kettles are visible, showing rust and wear on the white kettle and the plain metal base of the other.
description_view_3: The interior of the plain metal kettle is visible, with the sunflower motif partially seen on the white kettle in the background.
